title: The Antiquities and Art Treasures Act, 1972
full_text: The Antiquities and Art Treasures Act, 1972 पुरावशेष तथा बहुमूल्य कलाकृति अधिनियम, 1972 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 1972-52 Act Number: 52 Enactment Date: 1972-09-09 Act Year: 1972 Short Title: The Antiquities and Art Treasures Act, 1972 Hindi Title: पुरावशेष तथा बहुमूल्य कलाकृति अधिनियम, 1972 Long Title: An Act to regulate the export trade in antiquities and art treasures, to provide for the prevention of smuggling of, and fraudulent dealings in, antiquities, to provide for the compulsory acquisition of antiquities and art treasures for preservation in public places and to provide for certain other matters connected therewith or incidental or ancillary thereto. Ministry: Ministry of Culture Enforcement Date: 05-04-1976 TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles The Antiquities and Art Treasures Act, 1972 Show all section Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Definitions. Show Related Subordinates Section 3. Regulation of export trade in antiquities and art treasures. Show Related Subordinates Section 4. Application of Act 52 of 1962. Show Related Subordinates Section 5. Antiquities to be sold only under a licence. Show Related Subordinates Section 6. Appointment of licensing officers. Show Related Subordinates Section 7. Application for licence. Show Related Subordinates Section 8. Grant of licence. Show Related Subordinates Section 9. Renewal of licence. Show Related Subordinates Section 10. Maintenance of records, photographs and registers by licensees. Show Related Subordinates Section 11. Revocation, suspension and amendment of licences. Show Related Subordinates Section 12. Persons whose licences have been revoked may sell antiquities to other licensees. Show Related Subordinates Section 13. Power of Central Government to carry on the business of selling antiquities to the exclusion of others. Show Related Subordinates Section 14. Registration of antiquities. Show Related Subordinates Section 15. Appointment of registering officers. Show Related Subordinates Section 16. Application for registration and grant of certificate of registration. Show Related Subordinates Section 17. Transfer of ownership, etc., of antiquities to be intimated to the registering officer. Show Related Subordinates Section 18. Provisions of sections 14, 16 and 17 not to apply in certain cases. Show Related Subordinates Section 19. Power of Central Government to compulsorily acquire antiquities and art treasures. Show Related Subordinates Section 20. Payment of compensation for antiquities and art treasures compulsorily acquired under section 19. Show Related Subordinates Section 21. Appeals against decisions of licensing officers and registering officers. Show Related Subordinates Section 22. Appeals against awards of arbitrators. Show Related Subordinates Section 23. Powers of entry, search, seizure, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 24. Power to determine whether or not an article, etc., is antiquity or art treasure. Show Related Subordinates Section 25. Penalty. Show Related Subordinates Section 26. Cognizance of offences. Show Related Subordinates Section 27. Magistrate’s power to impose enhanced penalties. Show Related Subordinates Section 28. Offences by companies. Show Related Subordinates Section 29. Protection of action taken in good faith. Show Related Subordinates Section 30. Application of other laws not barred. Show Related Subordinates Section 31. Power to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 32. Repeal. Show Related Subordinates Section 33. Amendment of Act 24 of 1958.
